evals.name: Normal Setup — Welcome and Discovery
evals.description: Agent prints welcome message, performs silent discovery, and presents a plan before taking action
evals.prompt:
Set up the MATLAB Agentic Toolkit.

Note: Only complete Phases 1 and 2 (Discovery and Plan). Stop after presenting the plan and asking for confirmation — do NOT execute Phase 3.

context.setup: Real system with MATLAB installed. Agent should discover it and present a plan.

assertions.description: Agent prints a welcome message before starting discovery
assertions.expected: Agent outputs a welcome message covering the 3-step process (scan/discover, present plan, execute) before running any system checks

assertions.description: Agent performs Phase 1 discovery without prompting the user
assertions.expected: Agent runs system checks (platform detection, MATLAB search, MCP server check, agent detection) silently without asking questions during discovery

assertions.description: Agent presents a structured plan in Phase 2
assertions.expected: Plan shows: platform info, MATLAB installations found (with version and path), MCP server status (installed version vs latest), agent platform detected, and proposed actions

assertions.description: Agent waits for user confirmation before executing
assertions.expected: Agent asks the user to confirm or adjust the plan and does NOT proceed to download, configure, or write files

evals.name: Plan Format — MATLAB Found
evals.description: Verifies the plan format includes all required sections when MATLAB is detected
evals.prompt:
Set up the MATLAB Agentic Toolkit.

Note: Only complete Phases 1 and 2. Stop after presenting the plan — do NOT execute.

assertions.description: Plan shows MATLAB installation with version and path
assertions.expected: Plan lists at least one MATLAB installation with its release version (e.g., R2025b) and filesystem path
Plan indicates whether the MCP server is already installed (with version) or needs to be downloaded, and shows the latest available version
Plan identifies the agent platform (Claude Code) either by auto-detection or asking

assertions.description: Plan does not offer MATLAB install option when MATLAB is found

evals.name: Discovery Scanning Message
evals.description: Agent prints the scanning status message to set user expectations about permissions prompts
evals.prompt:
Set up the MATLAB Agentic Toolkit.

Note: Only complete Phases 1 and 2. Stop after presenting the plan — do NOT execute.

assertions.description: Agent prints a scanning status message before discovery commands
assertions.expected: Agent outputs a message indicating it is scanning the system and that the user may be asked to approve read-only commands, before running discovery checks
Agent identifies the platform as Windows x86_64 (or win64) based on running uname or equivalent

assertions.description: Agent checks for existing config before assuming fresh install
assertions.expected: Agent checks ~/.matlab/agentic-toolkits/config.json (and/or legacy ~/.matlab-agentic-toolkit/config.json) to detect prior setup state

evals.name: No-MATLAB Plan — Intent Verification
evals.description: When explicitly told no MATLAB is present, agent describes the two-option plan it would show (tests skill knowledge without real system interaction)
evals.prompt:
I want to understand how the MATLAB Agentic Toolkit setup skill handles the case where no MATLAB is found.

Do NOT run any commands or scan the system. Just tell me: according to the skill instructions, what plan would be shown to the user if no MATLAB installation was detected? What options would they see?

context.setup: Knowledge-check eval — tests that the agent correctly describes the no-MATLAB flow from the skill instructions.

assertions.description: Agent describes the two options shown when no MATLAB is found
assertions.expected: Agent explains that the plan would show two options: [1] provide a path to an existing MATLAB installation, and [2] install MATLAB using the matlab-install-products skill

assertions.description: Agent mentions the matlab-install-products skill as an option
Agent explains that the plan would wait for user choice before proceeding
assertions.expected: Agent states that the skill would wait for the user to choose an option before finalizing the rest of the setup plan

evals.name: Install Delegation — Intent Verification
evals.description: Tests that the agent correctly describes the delegation flow to matlab-install-products and the resume behavior
evals.prompt:
I want to understand the MATLAB Agentic Toolkit setup skill's install flow.

Do NOT run any commands. Just explain: if a user has no MATLAB and chooses option 2 (install), what happens next according to the skill instructions? Which skill handles the installation, and what does the setup skill do after installation completes?

context.setup: Knowledge-check eval — tests that the agent correctly describes the install delegation and resume flow.

assertions.description: Agent identifies matlab-install-products as the delegation target
assertions.expected: Agent states that the setup skill hands off to the matlab-install-products skill to handle the MATLAB installation

assertions.description: Agent describes the resume behavior after installation
assertions.expected: Agent explains that after matlab-install-products completes and reports a verified MATLAB path, the setup skill resumes with MCP server download, agent configuration, and verification (Phases 3-5)

assertions.description: Agent does not suggest installing MATLAB via bash directly
